b:T8d6d,<div class="specification">
<p>The pie chart shows the world demand for phosphorus in 2010. The fraction used as detergents and in industries has decreased in recent years in response to evidence that phosphate pollution of surface waters causes eutrophication.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe reasons that the availability of phosphates may become limiting to agriculture in the future.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain how eutrophication can occur from the excessive use of phosphates.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>c:T563,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. phosphate-rich rocks/resource is limited ✔ </p>
<p>b. demand exceeds availability of phosphorus <br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>there is greater consumption than P available ✔</p>
<p>c. cycling of phosphorus is slow ✔</p>
<p>d. as crops are removed, the phosphate levels within the soil is continually depleted ✔</p>
<p>e. there is no biological/industrial way to replenish the mineral «fast enough» <em>OWTTE</em><br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>«phosphate» fertilizers need to be added «to replenish it in the soil»<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>«lost phosphate» does not go back in the cycle ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. <span style="text-decoration: underline;">phosphates</span> are leached to lakes/rivers ✔</p>
<p>b. induces incremental growth of plants/bloom/algae ✔</p>
<p>c. algae do not let light go through water so less photosynthesis ✔</p>
<p>d. plants/algae die/decay ✔</p>
<p>e. bacterial decomposition consumes the oxygen ✔</p>
<p>f. creating state of hypoxia<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>greater biochemical oxygen demand ✔</p>
<p>g. causing death of «aerobic» organisms ✔<br/><em>eg: fish</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/c/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","c"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"18N.3.HL.TZ0.16"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"18N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","c-6-nitrogen-and-phosphorus-cycles"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
